title: The Burns And The Bees

jokes.text: Food fight! Take that, Ted Turner. In your face, founder of Facebook.
jokes.setup: Elite billionaires at sophisticated retreat
jokes.punchline: Acting like children in food fight with specific targets
jokes.explanation: Delivers on the ironic setup from Burns. The specific targets (Ted Turner, Facebook founder) add contemporary relevance but aren't deeply clever.

jokes.text: Skinny-dip! Yay! Yee-haw!
jokes.setup: Continuing the theme of billionaire childishness
jokes.punchline: Further escalation to naked swimming
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of the rich-people-acting-childish theme. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Men. The room for me.
jokes.setup: Burns sees a bathroom sign
jokes.punchline: His pompous way of reading 'Men's Room'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - his archaic, pretentious way of speaking applied to mundane signage. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Kearney, can you read it back? [Extended recursive reading]
jokes.setup: Jimbo asks Kearney to read back the dare
jokes.punchline: Kearney reads back everything including the request to read it back, creating infinite loop
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recursive comedy - very original concept executed well. The meta-humor of reading back the request to read back is clever and unexpected.

jokes.text: a dare has been placed on your nards. My nards accept.
jokes.setup: Formal dare challenge language
jokes.punchline: Using 'nards' in mock-formal context
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between formal language and crude slang. 'Nards' is juvenile but the formal structure makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Kearney reading back the entire conversation verbatim including dialogue tags
jokes.setup: Jimbo asks Kearney to 'read back' the dare
jokes.punchline: Kearney literally reads it like a script with character names and stage directions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor that breaks the fourth wall in an unexpected way. Very original and well-executed.

jokes.text: Stingamajigs!
jokes.setup: Children being attacked by bees
jokes.punchline: Childish made-up word for bees
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid-speak portmanteau. Shows how children would logically combine 'sting' and 'thingamajig'. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Other than the birth of my kid, this is my happiest moment.
jokes.setup: Willie observing the aftermath of bee attack
jokes.punchline: His joy at children's misfortune ranks just below his child's birth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie moment - his misanthropic joy contrasted with the one genuinely sweet thing (his child). Dark but in-character.

jokes.text: from Glasgow clear to Edinburgh
jokes.setup: Willie describing worldwide bee deaths
jokes.punchline: His 'worldwide' perspective is just across Scotland
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Willie's limited worldview. Glasgow to Edinburgh is maybe 50 miles, presented as a vast range.

jokes.text: Willie, I didn't know you were an apiarist. From context, I can tell that means beekeeper.
jokes.setup: Lisa uses sophisticated vocabulary
jokes.punchline: She explains it to the audience while pretending to deduce from context
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke acknowledging Lisa's vocabulary level while providing exposition naturally. Shows awareness of the show's own tropes.

jokes.text: 'Tis a tragedy, Lisa. No man should outlive his bees.
jokes.setup: Willie's emotional response to bee deaths
jokes.punchline: Treating bee lifespan like human relationships with dramatic gravity
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment with Willie's poetic Scottish sensibility applied to absurd situation.

jokes.text: They're stinging God now.
jokes.setup: Dead bees rising to heaven
jokes.punchline: Willie's matter-of-fact observation about celestial bee stings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark absurdism. Perfect economy - four words that create a vivid, funny, and slightly blasphemous image. Peak Willie deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Jeff Bezos wet his pants! I did not. That was apple juice from before.
jokes.setup: Burns announcing embarrassing fact about Bezos
jokes.punchline: Bezos's weak, specific denial
jokes.explanation: Solid embarrassment comedy. The 'apple juice from before' detail makes the denial worse by being so specific and implausible.

jokes.text: Bets don't talk. They see and raise.
jokes.setup: Smithers trying to speak while being bet in poker
jokes.punchline: Burns treating human beings like poker chips with poker terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy showing Burns's dehumanization. The poker terminology applied to humans is both clever wordplay and character-revealing.

jokes.text: Well, if we're betting people, I got a whole herd of them.
jokes.setup: Burns betting Smithers as a person
jokes.punchline: Cuban's casual reference to owning a 'herd' of people (basketball team)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the people-as-property theme. 'Herd' is perfect animal terminology for dehumanization while referring to a legitimate basketball team.

jokes.text: Don't let the forwards mix with the guards. And if they're acting sluggish, slip some steroids into their cocaine.
jokes.setup: Cuban giving Burns advice about caring for the basketball team
jokes.punchline: Escalating from segregation to casual drug mixing
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that gets funnier as it gets worse. The casual assumption that players already do cocaine is peak Simpsons satirical edge.

jokes.text: Ooh, now who will sting me and walk all over my sandwiches?
jokes.setup: Homer learning about bee crisis
jokes.punchline: His only concern is missing bee-related annoyances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the problems rather than benefits. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Flowers: the painted whores of the plant world.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining that bees pollinate flowers
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude metaphorical description of flowers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer observation that's both crude and botanically accurate. Flowers do 'advertise' for pollination. Perfect character voice and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Synthetic honey. Just like you remember.
jokes.setup: Advertisement for fake honey in bee-less future
jokes.punchline: Claiming synthetic product is 'just like you remember' real honey
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how advertising presents inferior substitutes. The phrase 'just like you remember' perfectly captures false nostalgia marketing.

jokes.text: D'oh! I never dreamed the future could be scary.
jokes.setup: Homer tasting terrible synthetic honey
jokes.punchline: His naive surprise that the future might be bad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer naivety - his optimistic worldview clashing with reality. The 'D'oh!' adds the classic Homer touch.

jokes.text: Let's go, Lisa. We've got some worms to save. Bees. Worms, bees, ticks, fleas. Let's go, go, go!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to be supportive of Lisa's environmental cause
jokes.punchline: His complete confusion about what exactly they're saving
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - enthusiastic but clueless. His grouping of random small creatures shows his lack of understanding while maintaining good intentions.

jokes.text: That's Associate Professor Aaron Ableman. How I hate him.
jokes.setup: Frink explaining he's not first in the phone book
jokes.punchline: His casual, intense hatred for the alphabetically superior scientist
jokes.explanation: Perfect academic pettiness - hating someone purely for alphabetical advantage. Shows how competitive and petty academics can be over trivial status.

jokes.text: Thundering thorax, it's just exactly what I feared!
jokes.setup: Frink examining the sick bee
jokes.punchline: His bee-themed exclamation replacing normal curse words
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific oath. Frink would naturally create scientific exclamations, and 'thorax' fits the bee theme perfectly.

jokes.text: Animals can get sick? I'm going to talk to the girl from now on.
jokes.setup: Homer's stupid question about animal illness
jokes.punchline: Frink's immediate decision to ignore Homer completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Homer's stupidity. Frink's matter-of-fact dismissal and preference for Lisa shows practical intelligence assessment.

jokes.text: Marry me, and I'll support you for life. You're not a bee! This thing is useless.
jokes.setup: Bee pheromone supposed to attract bees
jokes.punchline: Attracts Comic Book Guy instead with marriage proposal
jokes.explanation: Good subversion - pheromone works but on wrong species. Comic Book Guy's desperate proposal fits his pathetic romantic history perfectly.

jokes.text: Hold still, sweetie. Daddy will kill it. And this time, no screwups.
jokes.setup: Bee lands on Lisa and Homer prepares to kill it
jokes.punchline: His confident promise of no screwups implies a history of screwing up bee-killing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's track record of failure while trying to be reassuring. The confidence despite past failures is very Homer.

jokes.text: Yeah, much like that.
jokes.setup: Frink explains bee needs to sting someone, Lisa gets stung
jokes.punchline: Frink's casual confirmation as Lisa screams in pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to Lisa's pain. The casual academic tone contrasting with the physical suffering creates good dark comedy.

jokes.text: How long is that? Long enough that you will be known as Springfield's crazy bee girl.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks how long she needs to keep the bee colony
jokes.punchline: Frink's answer focuses on social consequences rather than time
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - instead of answering the time question, Frink provides the social consequence. Shows his scientific detachment from normal concerns.

jokes.text: Never being a grandmother. Really? I thought it was Lisa with a beard of bees.
jokes.setup: Homer remembers Marge's stated worst fear
jokes.punchline: His memory was wrong and oddly specific about bee beards
jokes.explanation: Good callback setup for the current situation. Homer's specific false memory of 'bee beard' perfectly predicts what's about to happen.

jokes.text: President Lincoln! You've come back, and you got rid of that hat.
jokes.setup: Homer sees Lisa with beard of bees
jokes.punchline: Mistakes her for beardless Lincoln
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy. Homer's brain makes the connection: beard = Lincoln, but he focuses on the missing stovepipe hat rather than the bee situation.

jokes.text: Stop Milhousing your sister.
jokes.setup: Bart making faces at Lisa with bee beard
jokes.punchline: Homer using 'Milhouse' as a verb for annoying behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant neologism. Using a character's name as a verb for their most annoying trait is perfect comedy writing. Very quotable and original.

jokes.text: All it takes is one troubled loner.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining bees rarely sting in clusters
jokes.punchline: Marge applying mass shooting psychology to bees
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant - applying human mass violence psychology to bee behavior. Shows how modern anxieties permeate everything, even bee safety.

jokes.text: Smithers, we're leading two to nothing. Run out the clock, boys. Run out the clock.
jokes.setup: Burns watching basketball for first time
jokes.punchline: Thinking 2-0 is a big lead in basketball and trying to run out clock
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns ignorance of modern sports. His ancient mindset applies baseball/football strategy to basketball inappropriately.

jokes.text: Big three in the Big D for the Big C. Mark Cuban... that's me.
jokes.setup: Cuban celebrating three-pointer enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: His forced alliteration and need to explain his own nickname
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of sports owner showboating. The forced alliteration and self-explanation shows ego, but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'm out of my mind! His money seems to have bought him happiness.
jokes.setup: Cuban acting crazy and enthusiastic
jokes.punchline: Burns's amazed realization that money can buy happiness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Burns - despite being rich, he's miserable and genuinely surprised that wealth can create joy. Shows his fundamental unhappiness.

jokes.text: Fun? Is that how it's pronounced? I've only seen it written.
jokes.setup: Cuban talking about having fun with the team
jokes.punchline: Burns has never encountered the concept of fun in practice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns characterization - so removed from normal human experience that 'fun' is purely academic. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: No one's paid attention to me for ten whole seconds.
jokes.setup: Cuban having conversation with Burns
jokes.punchline: His need for constant attention interrupting conversation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Cuban's attention-seeking personality.

jokes.text: You know, the feathers with the big eye that trick them into thinking that's my front? Said feathers are what I'll flaunt.
jokes.setup: Burns using peacock metaphor for showing off
jokes.punchline: His overly detailed, scientific explanation of peacock display behavior
jokes.explanation: Good Burns verbosity - he can't use a simple metaphor without explaining the biological mechanism. Shows his pedantic nature but goes on too long.

jokes.text: The secret is not to swallow the stingers.
jokes.setup: Marge eating pancakes with bees on them
jokes.punchline: Her practical advice about bee consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge practicality - treating bee consumption as just another cooking challenge. Her matter-of-fact tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: They're just drones, Lisa. They only live for, like, five minutes anyway. And they only have haploid cells, not diploid like me and Bart.
jokes.setup: Homer eating bees casually
jokes.punchline: His surprisingly accurate scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - Homer randomly knows complex biology. The contrast between casual bee-eating and genetic terminology is perfect.

jokes.text: They only have haploid cells, not diploid like me and Bart.
jokes.setup: Homer eating drone bees
jokes.punchline: Lisa using scientific terminology to justify Homer's bee consumption
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa character moment using science to rationalize absurd family behavior.

jokes.text: Which used to be a beautiful, thriving greenhouse till I was hired to run it.
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting abandoned greenhouse for bees
jokes.punchline: Admitting she's the reason it failed
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Marge moment. Her honest admission of failure is refreshingly direct and fits her self-deprecating nature.

jokes.text: Plus, there are flowers, and it's near a prison, so they'll have a place to sting people.
jokes.setup: Marge listing greenhouse benefits
jokes.punchline: Casually including prisoner-stinging as a feature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Marge moment - treating prisoner suffering as bee enrichment. The casual delivery makes the dark humor perfect.

jokes.text: You'll always have Bart. Always. But he'll be gone a lot repairing refrigerators. Always.
jokes.setup: Homer comforting Marge about Lisa leaving for college
jokes.punchline: His assumption Bart will become appliance repairman
jokes.explanation: Good dark family humor - Homer's low expectations for Bart delivered as comfort. The repetition of 'always' makes it both funny and sad.

jokes.text: You've seen him on the cover of the December 1943 issue of Collier's Magazine.
jokes.setup: Introducing Burns as new team owner
jokes.punchline: His last moment of fame was 60+ years ago in obscure magazine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns age joke - his celebrity peak was in the 1940s. The specific date and obscure magazine reference makes it more believable and funnier.

jokes.text: Springfield Excitement
jokes.setup: Burns renaming basketball team
jokes.punchline: Generic corporate name lacking any personality
jokes.explanation: Typical corporate rebranding joke showing Burns's disconnect from sports culture.

jokes.text: My country 'tis of thee, Austria, Hungary, Obey your king...
jokes.setup: Burns singing national anthem
jokes.punchline: Singing about Austrian-Hungarian Empire instead of America
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns historical confusion - he's so old his patriotic songs are about defunct empires. Perfect character integration and historically accurate confusion.

jokes.text: The European Union?! Good heavens! Ladies, hold onto your husbands!
jokes.setup: Burns learning about post-war history
jokes.punchline: His shock at European integration and bizarre warning to women
jokes.explanation: Good Burns reactionary response. His assumption that European cooperation threatens American marriages shows his outdated fears and prejudices.

jokes.text: Back to your brothel, harlots!
jokes.setup: Burns dismissing cheerleaders
jokes.punchline: Assuming they're prostitutes and ordering them away
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns misogyny and antiquated morality. His assumption about cheerleaders fits his Victorian mindset perfectly.

jokes.text: Sir, were those real bullets? Yes. Was that a real gorilla? No. I see.
jokes.setup: Burns's bizarre halftime entertainment involving shooting
jokes.punchline: The real bullets/fake gorilla distinction makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic - somehow fake gorilla is fine but real bullets are concerning. Smithers's deadpan 'I see' shows he's given up understanding Burns.

jokes.text: Time to think outside the boxeola.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to solve business problem
jokes.punchline: His antiquated slang addition to modern business phrase
jokes.explanation: Good Burns linguistic quirk - adding old-timey '-ola' suffix to modern business speak. Shows how he tries to modernize but can't escape his era.

jokes.text: That's it, men. Pave and tar the way God intended.
jokes.setup: Burns ordering construction of new arena
jokes.punchline: Treating environmental destruction as divine plan
jokes.explanation: Good Burns line showing his twisted environmentally destructive worldview.

jokes.text: Kill his acorns and make him watch.
jokes.setup: Squirrel protesting Burns's construction project
jokes.punchline: Burns's disproportionately cruel response to animal protest
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Burns applies mafia-style intimidation to a squirrel. The psychological torture aspect ('make him watch') is perfectly evil Burns.

jokes.text: I don't know who that guy was you were leaving your kids with.
jokes.setup: Town meeting about childcare
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of unsafe childcare practices
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor about parental negligence.

jokes.text: The feisty pig of the desert? Apparently not feisty enough.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions failed javelina conservation
jokes.punchline: Crowd member's dismissive comment about javelina's fighting spirit
jokes.explanation: Good environmental activism satire. The crowd's dismissive attitude toward extinct species shows public apathy toward conservation efforts.

jokes.text: Muk Mu, the human North Pole.
jokes.setup: Burns describing his draft pick
jokes.punchline: Bizarre description of athlete as geographical feature
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist description. 'Human North Pole' suggests both size and coldness while being completely nonsensical as athletic description.

jokes.text: No, I'm afraid he'll be on the first kayak back to Ketchikan.
jokes.setup: Burns continuing about losing Muk Mu
jokes.punchline: Assuming Alaskan travels by kayak
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment showing his stereotypical thinking about Alaska.

jokes.text: Please, don't take our freak! I love you, Muk Mu!
jokes.setup: Crowd learning Muk Mu is leaving
jokes.punchline: Their emotional attachment to someone they call a 'freak'
jokes.explanation: Good Springfield crowd psychology - simultaneously dehumanizing and loving their entertainment. Shows how they view unusual people as spectacle.

jokes.text: I saw this ad in a gentleman's magazine for excited African honeys, and that's what they sent me.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy explaining how he got aggressive bees
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding adult magazine terminology, expecting women but getting bees
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre subversion - Comic Book Guy's naivety about adult advertising leads to accidental bee acquisition. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: And she's ready for a night of anonymous sex with multiple partners.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy handling queen bee for breeding
jokes.punchline: Describing bee mating in human sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Good adult humor disguised as bee biology. The clinical description of bee reproduction using human relationship terms is appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: Now, let's give them some privacy while they... get down to buzziness.
jokes.setup: Leaving bees to mate privately
jokes.punchline: Bee-themed pun on 'business'
jokes.explanation: Standard pun - predictable wordplay that fits the situation but isn't particularly clever. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: If they was me, they'd be done by now.
jokes.setup: Waiting for bees to finish mating
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing his sexual stamina to bees
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer oversharing moment with good character integration.

jokes.text: I've never seen you walk up an incline before.
jokes.setup: Homer walking uphill to show Lisa something special
jokes.punchline: Lisa's observation of Homer's unprecedented physical effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa observation about Homer's laziness. The scientific term 'incline' instead of 'hill' shows her vocabulary while highlighting Homer's minimal activity level.

jokes.text: Your bees died days ago. These are their angry, mutant descendants.
jokes.setup: Lisa excited to see her bees
jokes.punchline: Homer casually revealing original bees are dead, replaced by aggressive hybrids
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy revelation - Homer presents terrible news as a gift. The casual delivery of pet death followed by 'mutant descendants' is perfectly Homer.

jokes.text: Are you guys dangerous? Ooh!
jokes.setup: Homer asking bees if they're dangerous
jokes.punchline: Gets immediately stung while asking
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate payoff - Homer gets instant answer to his question. Predictable but well-executed timing and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Are you guys dangerous? Ooh! A simple yes would have sufficed.
jokes.setup: Homer casually asking dangerous bees about their nature
jokes.punchline: Getting stung immediately after asking
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Homer's typical poor decision-making.

jokes.text: To that new stadium that looks exactly like a hive.
jokes.setup: Wondering where angry bees will go
jokes.punchline: Stadium's design accidentally attracts the very problem it caused
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for final confrontation with nice visual logic.

jokes.text: Stop! I am one of you!
jokes.setup: Burns being attacked by killer bees
jokes.punchline: Appeals to bees as fellow evil creature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns self-awareness - he recognizes his kinship with aggressive, swarming creatures. Shows he knows exactly what he is.

jokes.text: My microphone is no microphone at all. It's bees! Am I being badly stung? Yes! And it hurts!
jokes.setup: Announcer trying to report on bee invasion
jokes.punchline: His microphone is made of bees, continues reporting while being stung
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - professional broadcaster continues reporting his own bee attack. The revelation that his microphone is bees is wonderfully surreal.

jokes.text: Our bullets are useless. Aah! Okay, they're useful on me.
jokes.setup: Police shooting at bees ineffectively
jokes.punchline: Immediately shoots himself, proving bullets work on humans
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy timing - the immediate contradiction of 'useless bullets' followed by self-injury. Great economy and unexpected turn.

jokes.text: when next we meet, the beekeeper will be the beekeepee!
jokes.setup: Burns retreating from bee defeat
jokes.punchline: His threat using fake grammatical construction
jokes.explanation: Good Burns linguistic invention - creating 'beekeepee' as passive form. Shows his intelligence and pompous speech patterns while being ridiculous.

jokes.text: He's not a billionaire at all! Wait, wait, wait. It's only a matter of four million. I'm sure I can find that somewhere.
jokes.setup: Burns revealed to be worth only $996 million
jokes.punchline: His desperate attempt to minimize the $4 million shortfall
jokes.explanation: Good wealth inequality comedy - $4 million is 'only' to Burns but life-changing to normal people. Shows the absurd scale of billionaire wealth.

jokes.text: No! No! Just kill me now!
jokes.setup: Burns being relegated to millionaires' camp
jokes.punchline: Preferring death to lower wealth status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns priorities - social status among ultra-wealthy matters more than life itself. Shows his complete detachment from normal values.

jokes.text: Howdy do? I own a minor league hockey team.
jokes.setup: Burns meeting fellow millionaires
jokes.punchline: The vast difference in scale between billionaire and millionaire assets
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing wealth tiers - going from NBA team to minor league hockey represents Burns's fall from grace in rich people hierarchy.

tmdb_overview: Mr Burns wins a professional basketball team in a poker game at the annual billionaire's retreat. He decides to build a state-of-the-art facility in an attempt to lure fans, but the stadium site is on the last remaining bee colony in Springfield - prompting Lisa to lead a protest.
